MOUNT OUSLEY INTERCHANGE PROJECT

Motorists are advised of changed traffic conditions while work is carried out on the Mount Ousley interchange project.

Road shoulder, single lane closures and stop/slow traffic conditions will be in place as required on:

  • M1 Princes Mwy between Mount Ousley Rd and Northfields Ave
  • Mount Ousley Rd
  • Irvine St
  • Northfields Ave (including northbound M1 Princes Mwy on and off ramps)
  • Dumfries Ave

During the work, traffic control and reduced speed limits will be in place at times.

More information is available on the project webpage.

arrangementElements.html:
All heavy vehicles up to and including PBS 1 can use the southern heavy vehicle route via Tompsitt Dr, Edwin Land Pkwy and Ellerton Dr.

All OSOM vehicles over PBS 2 to use the existing approved OSOM route via Pialligo Rd and Yass Rd.

Please note:

Unless specified otherwise, operators of Class 1,2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable approved alternate route. OSOM vehicles operating under the National Class 1 Load Carrying Vehicles Mass and/or Dimension Exemption Notices can use the available detours if they are suitable for their combination. Operators are reminded that under Schedule 8 of the Heavy Vehicle (Mass, Dimension and Loading) National Regulation (NSW), there is a requirement for the driver and operator to scope the proposed route prior to travel to ensure it is suitable for their vehicle.

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.

Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time.

Lane closures, intermittent stopping and reduced speed limit of 40km/h in place during work hours (Mon - Fri, 7am to 6pm and Sat, 8am to 1pm). The speed limit outside work hours is 60km/h, with sections of 80km/h on approach. Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time.
Alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place to allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time. Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.
Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time.

﻿Picton Road eastbound will be closed from 7.30pm to ensure the M1 Princes Motorway is clear by 8pm. 

M1 Princes Motorway (Mount Ousley) between Princes Highway (Bulli Pass) and Memorial Drive will also have closures in place on these nights.
properties.diversions:
Motorists travelling between Wollongong and the Hume Motorway will be detoured via Memorial Drive, Bulli Pass and Appin Road, and should allow up to 40 minutes additional travel time.

Light vehicles can also use Wilton Road (Broughton Pass) between Appin Road and Picton Road or the Illawarra Highway (Macquarie Pass).

Motorists travelling between Wollongong and Bulli Tops will be detoured via Memorial Drive and Bulli Pass and should allow up to 15 minutes additional travel time.

These detours are not suitable for any OSOM vehicle or any vehicle exceeding 19 metres and/or 50.5 tonnes. Operators of these vehicles are advised to delay their journey as no alternative detour is available.

properties.otherAdvice: Picton Road between Almond Street and M1 Princes Motorway will also have closures in place on these nights.
properties.diversions:
Motorists travelling between Wollongong and Bulli Tops will be detoured via Memorial Drive and Bulli Pass and should allow up to 15 minutes additional travel time. 

Motorists travelling between Wollongong and the Hume Motorway can detour via Memorial Drive, Bulli Pass, Appin Road, or the Illawarra Highway (Macquarie Pass).

These detours are not suitable for any OSOM vehicle or any vehicle exceeding 19 metres and/or 50.5 tonnes. Operators of these vehicles are advised to delay their journey as no alternative detour is available.

arrangementElements.html:
Vehicles longer than 7.5 metres or heavier than 12 tonnes will be detoured via the Illawarra Highway and Princes Highway, adding 90 minutes of extra travel time. Please note Macquarie Pass is only suitable for PBS Level 1 vehicles.

Please review the Restricted Access Vehicles map to help you plan an alternative route if required.
arrangementElements.title: Advice for OSOM Operators

properties.diversions:
Motorists with vehicles less than 7.5 metres long and under 12 tonnes can detour via Kangaroo Valley Road.

All other vehicles will be detoured via the Illawarra Highway and Princes Highway, adding 90 minutes to travel times.
